content:
Customize the Nuxt UI theme to create a branded look with the following requirements:

1. **Primary color**: Change the primary color to `indigo` using `app.config.ts`
2. **Secondary color**: Set secondary to `rose`
3. **Custom font**: In the CSS file, use the `@theme` directive to set a custom sans font family to `'Inter', sans-serif`
4. **Demo page**: Create a page that showcases the theme with:
   - A `UButton` with `color="primary"` showing the indigo theme
   - A `UButton` with `color="secondary"` showing the rose theme
   - A `UCard` component wrapping some content
   - A `UBadge` component

The key requirement is using Nuxt UI v4's CSS-first theming approach with the `@theme` directive in CSS and `app.config.ts` for color configuration.

message.content:
Customize the Nuxt UI theme to create a branded look with the following requirements:

1. **Primary color**: Change the primary color to `indigo` using `app.config.ts`
2. **Secondary color**: Set secondary to `rose`
3. **Custom font**: In the CSS file, use the `@theme` directive to set a custom sans font family to `'Inter', sans-serif`
4. **Demo page**: Create a page that showcases the theme with:
   - A `UButton` with `color="primary"` showing the indigo theme
   - A `UButton` with `color="secondary"` showing the rose theme
   - A `UCard` component wrapping some content
   - A `UBadge` component

The key requirement is using Nuxt UI v4's CSS-first theming approach with the `@theme` directive in CSS and `app.config.ts` for color configuration.

1	# Installation
2	
3	\> \[!NOTE]
4	\> See: /docs/getting-started/installation/vue
5	\> Looking for the Vue version?
6	
7	## Setup
8	
9	### Add to a Nuxt project
10	
11	Install the Nuxt UI package
12	\`\`\`bash
13	pnpm add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
14	\`\`\`
15	\`\`\`bash
16	yarn add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
17	\`\`\`
18	\`\`\`bash
19	npm install @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
20	\`\`\`
21	\`\`\`bash
22	bun add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
23	\`\`\`
24	Add the Nuxt UI module in your nuxt.config.ts
25	\`\`\`ts
26	export default defineNuxtConfig({
27	modules: \['@nuxt/ui']
28	})
29	\`\`\`
30	Import Tailwind CSS and Nuxt UI in your CSS
31	\`\`\`css
32	@import "tailwindcss";
33	@import "@nuxt/ui";
34	\`\`\`
35	\`\`\`ts
36	export default defineNuxtConfig({
37	modules: \['@nuxt/ui'],
38	css: \['\~/assets/css/main.css']
39	})
40	\`\`\`
41	\> \[!TIP]
42	\> See: https\://nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/layers
43	\> When using \[Nuxt Layers]\(https\://nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/layers), the module automatically generates \[\`@source\`]\(https\://tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives#source-directive) directives for each layer directory, ensuring Tailwind CSS scans all your layer source files for utility classes.
44	\> \[!NOTE]
45	\> It's recommended to install the \[Tailwind CSS IntelliSense]\(https\://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss) extension for VSCode and add the following settings:
46	\> \`\`\`json
47	\> {
48	\> "files.associations": {
49	\> "\*.css": "tailwindcss"
50	\> },
51	\> "editor.quickSuggestions": {
52	\> "strings": "on"
53	\> },
54	\> "tailwindCSS.classAttributes": \["class", "ui"],
55	\> "tailwindCSS.classFunctions": \["defineAppConfig"]
56	\> }
57	\>
58	\> \`\`\`
59	Wrap your app with App component
60	\`\`\`vue
61	\<template>
62	\<UApp>
63	\<NuxtPage />
64	\</UApp>
65	\</template>
66	\`\`\`
67	\> \[!NOTE]
68	\> See: /docs/components/app
69	\> The \`App\` component provides global configurations and is required for Toast, Tooltip components to work as well as Programmatic Overlays.
70	
71	### Use a Nuxt template
72	
73	Get started with one of our [official templates](https://ui.nuxt.com/templates) by using the `Use this template` button on GitHub or the CLI:
74	
75	\`\`\`bash
76	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui
77	\`\`\`
78	\`\`\`bash
79	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/landing
80	\`\`\`
81	\`\`\`bash
82	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/docs
83	\`\`\`
84	\`\`\`bash
85	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/saas
86	\`\`\`
87	\`\`\`bash
88	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/dashboard
89	\`\`\`
90	\`\`\`bash
91	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/chat
92	\`\`\`
93	\`\`\`bash
94	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/portfolio
95	\`\`\`
96	\`\`\`bash
97	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/changelog
98	\`\`\`
99	\`\`\`bash
100	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/editor

file.content:
# Installation

\> \[!NOTE]
\> See: /docs/getting-started/installation/vue
\> Looking for the Vue version?

## Setup

### Add to a Nuxt project

Install the Nuxt UI package
\`\`\`bash
pnpm add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
yarn add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm install @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
bun add @nuxt/ui tailwindcss
\`\`\`
Add the Nuxt UI module in your nuxt.config.ts
\`\`\`ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
modules: \['@nuxt/ui']
})
\`\`\`
Import Tailwind CSS and Nuxt UI in your CSS
\`\`\`css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "@nuxt/ui";
\`\`\`
\`\`\`ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
modules: \['@nuxt/ui'],
css: \['\~/assets/css/main.css']
})
\`\`\`
\> \[!TIP]
\> See: https\://nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/layers
\> When using \[Nuxt Layers]\(https\://nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/layers), the module automatically generates \[\`@source\`]\(https\://tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives#source-directive) directives for each layer directory, ensuring Tailwind CSS scans all your layer source files for utility classes.
\> \[!NOTE]
\> It's recommended to install the \[Tailwind CSS IntelliSense]\(https\://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=bradlc.vscode-tailwindcss) extension for VSCode and add the following settings:
\> \`\`\`json
\> {
\> "files.associations": {
\> "\*.css": "tailwindcss"
\> },
\> "editor.quickSuggestions": {
\> "strings": "on"
\> },
\> "tailwindCSS.classAttributes": \["class", "ui"],
\> "tailwindCSS.classFunctions": \["defineAppConfig"]
\> }
\>
\> \`\`\`
Wrap your app with App component
\`\`\`vue
\<template>
\<UApp>
\<NuxtPage />
\</UApp>
\</template>
\`\`\`
\> \[!NOTE]
\> See: /docs/components/app
\> The \`App\` component provides global configurations and is required for Toast, Tooltip components to work as well as Programmatic Overlays.

### Use a Nuxt template

Get started with one of our [official templates](https://ui.nuxt.com/templates) by using the `Use this template` button on GitHub or the CLI:

\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/landing
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/docs
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/saas
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/dashboard
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/chat
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/portfolio
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/changelog
\`\`\`
\`\`\`bash
npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/editor

100	npm create nuxt\@latest -- -t ui/editor
101	\`\`\`
102	
103	\*\*Starter\*\*
104	A minimal template to get started with Nuxt UI.
105	\*\*Landing\*\*
106	A modern landing page template powered by Nuxt Content.
107	\*\*Docs\*\*
108	A documentation template powered by Nuxt Content.
109	\*\*SaaS\*\*
110	A SaaS template with landing, pricing, docs and blog powered by Nuxt Content.
111	\*\*Dashboard\*\*
112	A dashboard template with multi-column layout for building sophisticated admin interfaces.
113	\*\*Chat\*\*
114	An AI chatbot template to build your own chatbot powered by Vercel AI SDK.
115	\*\*Portfolio\*\*
116	A sleek portfolio template to showcase your work, skills and blog powered by Nuxt Content.
117	\*\*Changelog\*\*
118	A changelog template to display your repository releases notes from GitHub powered by Nuxt MDC.
119	\*\*Editor\*\*
120	A rich text editor template powered by TipTap with support for markdown, HTML, and JSON content types.
121	
122	## Options
123	
124	You can customize Nuxt UI by providing options in your `nuxt.config.ts`.
125	
126	### `prefix`
127	
128	Use the `prefix` option to change the prefix of the components.
129	
130	- Default: `U`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
131	
132	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-6}
133	export default defineNuxtConfig({
134	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
135	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
136	  ui: {
137	    prefix: 'Nuxt'
138	  }
139	})
140	```
141	
142	### `fonts`
143	
144	Use the `fonts` option to enable or disable the [`@nuxt/fonts`](https://github.com/nuxt/fonts){rel=""nofollow""} module.
145	
146	- Default: `true`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
147	
148	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-6}
149	export default defineNuxtConfig({
150	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
151	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
152	  ui: {
153	    fonts: false
154	  }
155	})
156	```
157	
158	### `colorMode`
159	
160	Use the `colorMode` option to enable or disable the [`@nuxt/color-mode`](https://github.com/nuxt-modules/color-mode){rel=""nofollow""} module.
161	
162	- Default: `true`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
163	
164	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-6}
165	export default defineNuxtConfig({
166	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
167	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
168	  ui: {
169	    colorMode: false
170	  }
171	})
172	```
173	
174	### `theme.colors`
175	
176	Use the `theme.colors` option to define the dynamic color aliases used to generate components theme.
177	
178	- Default: `['primary', 'secondary', 'success', 'info', 'warning', 'error']`{.inline,language-ts-type,shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
179	
180	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-8}
181	export default defineNuxtConfig({
182	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
183	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
184	  ui: {
185	    theme: {
186	      colors: ['primary', 'error']
187	    }
188	  }
189	})
190	```
191	
192	\> \[!TIP]
193	\> See: /docs/getting-started/theme/design-system#colors
194	\> Learn more about color customization and theming in the Theme section.
195	
196	### `theme.transitions`
197	
198	Use the `theme.transitions` option to enable or disable transitions on components.
199	
200	- Default: `true`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
201	
202	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-8}
203	export default defineNuxtConfig({
204	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
205	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
206	  ui: {
207	    theme: {
208	      transitions: false
209	    }
210	  }
211	})
212	```
213	
214	\> \[!NOTE]
215	\> This option adds the \`transition-colors\` class on components with hover or active states.
216	
217	### `theme.defaultVariants`
218	
219	Use the `theme.defaultVariants` option to override the default `color` and `size` variants for components.
220	
221	- Default: `{ color: 'primary', size: 'md' }`{.shiki,shiki-themes,material-theme-lighter,material-theme,material-theme-palenight lang="ts-type"}
222	
223	```ts [nuxt.config.ts] {4-11}
224	export default defineNuxtConfig({
225	  modules: ['@nuxt/ui'],
226	  css: ['~/assets/css/main.css'],
227	  ui: {
228	    theme: {
229	      defaultVariants: {
230	        color: 'neutral',
231	        size: 'sm'
232	      }
233	    }
234	  }
235	})
236	```
237	
238	### `theme.prefix` `4.2+`
239	
240	Use the `theme.prefix` option to configure the same prefix you set on your Tailwind CSS import. This ensures Nuxt UI components use the correct prefixed utility classes and CSS variables.
241	
242	\`\`\`ts
243	export default defineNuxtConfig({
244	modules: \['@nuxt/ui'],
245	css: \['\~/assets/css/main.css'],
246	ui: {
247	theme: {
248	prefix: 'tw'
249	}

2812:In Nuxt UI v2, we had a mix between a design system with `primary`, `gray`, `error` aliases and all the colors from Tailwind CSS. We've replaced it with a proper [design system](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/theme/design-system) with 7 color aliases:
2816:| `primary`{color="primary"}     | `green`  | Main brand color, used as the default color for components. |
2817:| `secondary`{color="secondary"} | `blue`   | Secondary color to complement the primary color.            |
2872:- The color configuration in `app.config.ts` has been moved into a `colors` object:
2877:-   primary: 'green',
2880:+     primary: 'green',
2889:Nuxt UI components are now styled using the [Tailwind Variants API](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/theme/components), which makes all the overrides you made using the `app.config.ts` and the `ui` prop obsolete.
2891:- Update your [`app.config.ts`](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/theme/components#global-config) to override components with their new theme:

2812:In Nuxt UI v2, we had a mix between a design system with `primary`, `gray`, `error` aliases and all the colors from Tailwind CSS. We've replaced it with a proper [design system](https://ui.nuxt.com/docs/getting-started/theme/design-system) with 7 color aliases:
2816:| `primary`{color="primary"}     | `green`  | Main brand color, used as the default color for components. |
2817:| `secondary`{color="secondary"} | `blue`   | Secondary color to complement the primary color.            |

content.thinking: Let me read the theming section of the docs more carefully, especially around lines 3782-3940.

content.content:
3900	\> Try the theme picker in the header to instantly see how different color schemes affect the entire UI!
3901	
3902	### Runtime configuration
3903	
3904	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
3905	You can configure these colors at runtime in your app.config.ts file under the ui.colors key, allowing for dynamic theme customization without restarting the server:
3906	\`\`\`ts
3907	export default defineAppConfig({
3908	ui: {
3909	colors: {
3910	primary: 'blue',
3911	secondary: 'purple',
3912	neutral: 'zinc'
3913	}
3914	}
3915	})
3916	\`\`\`
3917	\*\*Vue:\*\*
3918	You can configure these colors at runtime in your vite.config.ts file under the ui.colors key, allowing for dynamic theme customization:
3919	\`\`\`ts
3920	import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
3921	import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
3922	import ui from '@nuxt/ui/vite'
3923	export default defineConfig({
3924	plugins: \[
3925	vue(),
3926	ui({
3927	ui: {
3928	colors: {
3929	primary: 'blue',
3930	secondary: 'purple',
3931	neutral: 'zinc'
3932	}
3933	}
3934	})
3935	]
3936	})
3937	\`\`\`
3938	
3939	\> \[!CAUTION]
3940	\> You can only use colors that exist in your theme. Either\:Use \[Tailwind's default colors]\(https\://tailwindcss.com/docs/colors) (like \`blue\`, \`green\`, \`zinc\`)Define custom colors first using the \`@theme\` directive (like \`brand\` in our example above)
3941	
3942	### Extend colors
3943	
3944	You may want to define extra semantic colors beyond the defaults, such as adding a `tertiary` color:
3945	
3946	\*\*Nuxt:\*\*
3947	First, register the new color in your nuxt.config.ts under the ui.theme.colors key:
3948	\`\`\`ts
3949	export default defineNuxtConfig({
3950	ui: {
3951	theme: {
3952	colors: \[
3953	'primary',
3954	'secondary',
3955	'tertiary',
3956	'info',
3957	'success',
3958	'warning',
3959	'error'
3960	]
3961	}
3962	}
3963	})
3964	\`\`\`
3965	Then, assign it in your app.config.ts under the ui.colors key:
3966	\`\`\`ts
3967	export default defineAppConfig({
3968	ui: {
3969	colors: {
3970	primary: 'blue',
3971	secondary: 'purple',
3972	tertiary: 'indigo'
3973	}
3974	}
3975	})
3976	\`\`\`
3977	\*\*Vue:\*\*
3978	Register and assign the new color in your vite.config.ts file:
3979	\`\`\`ts

lines:
+

This card showcases the Nuxt UI v4 theming with indigo as the primary color and rose as the secondary color.

content.text:
Done. Here's what was set up:

1. **`app/app.config.ts`** — created with `ui.colors.primary: 'indigo'` and `ui.colors.secondary: 'rose'` for runtime color configuration.

2. **`app/assets/css/main.css`** — added `@theme { --font-sans: 'Inter', sans-serif; }` using the CSS-first theming approach.

3. **`app/pages/index.vue`** — demo page with:
   - `UButton color="primary"` (indigo)
   - `UButton color="secondary"` (rose)
   - `UCard` with header/footer slots
   - Two `UBadge` components (primary + secondary) in the card footer
